sourceCluster.label: BLS Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey

composite.summary: Maine's workforce conditions show rising strain. Many workers are carrying added pressure that can show up as burnout, turnover, or reduced capacity. The signal suggests the risks are manageable, but they are starting to stack across daily stability, health, and opportunity. Focused changes can reduce friction and restore breathing room. The strongest pressure points are showing up in belonging and day to day stability.

Social.title: Social
Social.signalSummary: Social signals show rising pressure, with a score near 55.5. Focused intervention can prevent risk accumulation.
employerImplications: Belonging and trust influence retention and attendance stability.
Manager consistency shapes daily team cohesion.
Workplace relationships affect referral pipelines and local reputation.
recommendedActions: Build manager routines for psychological safety check-ins.
Run short pulse loops on belonging and respond within one cycle.
Support peer mentoring for early tenure workers.

Purpose.title: Purpose
Purpose.signalSummary: Purpose signals show rising pressure, with a score near 46.6. Focused intervention can prevent risk accumulation.

metrics.label: Average Weekly Earnings, Private Sector

metrics.label: Job Openings Rate
metrics.sourceLabel: BLS Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey

metrics.label: Private Wage and Salary Worker Share

employerImplications: Role clarity improves engagement and on-the-job judgment.
Visible mission alignment strengthens persistence in hard periods.
Recognition systems affect whether workers see a path to stay.
recommendedActions: Translate strategy into role-level impact statements each quarter.
Use practical recognition tied to contribution and learning.
Align manager one-on-ones to purpose and growth checkpoints.

Achievement.title: Achievement
Achievement.signalSummary: Achievement signals show rising pressure, with a score near 58.7. Focused intervention can prevent risk accumulation.
employerImplications: Advancement pathways influence retention in competitive labor markets.
Skill transfer quality affects output and error recovery.
Compensation progression signals whether effort is rewarded fairly.
recommendedActions: Define progression ladders with transparent criteria.
Pair training with supervised practice and feedback loops.
Review wage compression risk in high-turnover roles.

Risk.title: Risk
Risk.signalSummary: Risk signals are stable, with a score near 66.5. Conditions are workable, with specific opportunities for improvement.

metrics.label: Layoffs and Discharges Rate
metrics.sourceLabel: BLS Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey

employerImplications: Housing and financial pressure influence attendance and turnover.
Health burden can reduce capacity even when vacancy levels are stable.
Early risk signals can prevent avoidable operational disruption.
recommendedActions: Create workload triage triggers for peak pressure periods.
Expand employee resource navigation for housing and health support.
Track early absenteeism clusters and respond at team level.

Knowledge.title: Knowledge
Knowledge.signalSummary: Knowledge signals show rising pressure, with a score near 64.6. Focused intervention can prevent risk accumulation.

metrics.label: Adults 25+ With Bachelor's Degree or Higher
US Education and Health Services Employment (Monthly)
Adults 25+ With High School Diploma Or Higher

employerImplications: Learning access affects long-term competitiveness and adaptation.
Digital access and credential pathways shape recruitment reach.
Innovation capacity depends on time for upskilling and problem solving.
recommendedActions: Protect scheduled learning time in core roles.
Partner with apprenticeship and credential providers in Maine.
Tie learning outcomes to operational improvement metrics.

methodology.scoreCalculation: SPARK combines five pillars: Social, Purpose, Achievement, Risk, and Knowledge. Scores are normalized to a 0 to 100 scale and then combined into a composite signal.
methodology.recencyRules: Metrics are direction-adjusted and normalized by documented rules. Higher-is-better and lower-is-better behavior is applied consistently by metric metadata.
methodology.missingDataRule: If a metric is missing or stale, it is flagged in quality reporting and excluded from high-confidence interpretations when appropriate.
